name: N-acetylcysteine
headline: NAC
dek: N-acetylcysteine is the most efficient route to raising endogenous glutathione, and a well-studied molecule in its own right. Modest acne and inflammation data; mood and trichotillomania endpoints; safer profile than IV glutathione at a fraction of the price.
subheadB: Better evidence than the parent molecule.
bottom: A useful, defensible, inexpensive supplement with a respectable evidence base. The smart pivot from oral glutathione for anyone interested in the antioxidant pathway.

evidence.note: Modest reduction in lesion count over 12 weeks. Useful adjunct alongside topicals.
Off-label but well-supported indication. Reduces hair-pulling urges over 12 weeks.
The most efficient route to raising endogenous glutathione status. The science here is solid.
Useful in PCOS-driven acne and hirsutism, often in combination with myo-inositol.

forms.note: Standard form. Twice daily on an empty stomach for skin endpoints.
Once-daily convenience. Equivalent endpoints in extended-release trials.
Used in respiratory indications. Sulfur taste is the limiter; capsules are easier.

faq.q: Is it safer than IV glutathione?
faq.a: Substantially. NAC has been used clinically for decades (paracetamol overdose, mucolytic in respiratory disease) at doses higher than the skin protocol. The safety profile is well-mapped, the cost is low, and the gut-bypass concerns of oral glutathione are sidestepped via the precursor route.
8–12 weeks for inflammatory acne adjunct work. The glutathione biomarker shift is faster (4–6 weeks), but skin endpoints lag the biochemistry.
NAC reduces nitroglycerin tolerance and may interact with anticoagulants at high doses. Most other interactions are minor. Discuss with your clinician if you take cardiovascular medication.

There is genuine, replicated literature on NAC in OCD-spectrum conditions, trichotillomania, and depression as adjunct. The skin-mood connection is real for inflammatory acne; whether NAC drives benefit through skin or mood pathways is unclear.
